passage.title: Tablet Station Redesign
passage.text: The technology team wanted younger students to follow tablet station steps more easily. At first, the team made one poster with many app icons and long instructions, but students tapped the wrong app because the directions were crowded. A first-grade test group said "I see many pictures, but I still do not know which app to open first." After hearing that, the team split each tech task into one card per step and placed the same card beside the real tablet station. They also set a quick-help board near the charging shelf so students could compare the sign with the real tool right away. On the next test day, more students completed the tablet path without teacher help on the next test. The team noticed that one clear tech step at a time worked better than crowded instructions. Because of that result, the team decided to redesign more tech stations with the same clear format.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Writing long reports about class rules
Improving technology directions so younger students can follow them
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: Students tapped the wrong app because directions were crowded
The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the team place beside the real tablet station?
choices: Larger arrows
Short signs
Color blocks
The same card
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does crowded mean in the second sentence?
choices: A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
Too full and hard to use quickly
A room that is easy to clean
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It removed every sign from the area
The new design showed one tech action more clearly at a time
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Use the same clear format on more tech stations
Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
